metadata.purpose: List of credible venues (conferences, special tracks, and journals) at the intersection of computing and international development / sustainability / social impact, with links to each year's proceedings or issue index for roughly the last five years (2021-2026). This is a venue-level index, not a paper-level index -- the next step is to drill into each linked proceedings/issue page and pull individual papers into readings.json.
metadata.credibility_note: Per instruction: any ACM-affiliated venue is treated as credible by default. IEEE venues are included only when clearly international in scope. Journals are included based on a combination of publisher reputation, editorial board affiliations (leading universities), and standing in the field, rather than impact factor alone. A few venues are flagged as broader/general venues that merely have a strong ICTD-relevant subset, rather than being dedicated ICTD venues -- for those, the main proceedings link is given but will need keyword/topic filtering to extract the relevant subset (same treatment as KDD and the AAAI main proceedings).
metadata.pipeline_note: Proceedings/issue URLs below match src/venue_configs.py (the scrape targets used by run_pipeline.py). Where ACM DL or IEEE Xplore blocks bots, DBLP or publisher-native URLs are used instead.
coverage_caveats: Some venues are not annual (e.g. ACM ICTD and IFIP ICT4D run roughly every 1-2 years). Years with no edition are simply omitted rather than padded.

For very high-volume general venues (KDD, CHI, CSCW, AAAI main proceedings, Climate Change AI workshop papers), the link given is to the venue/year index rather than to individual papers, because the development/sustainability-relevant subset is a small fraction of the total and needs manual or keyword-filtered drill-down -- flagged per-venue with 'needs_filtering': true.
AAAI's 'Special Track on AI for Social Impact' (AISI) is sometimes published as its own issue and sometimes folded into a combined 'Special Tracks' issue -- noted per year below.

venues.name: ACM SIGCAS/SIGCHI Conference on Computing and Sustainable Societies (COMPASS)
venues.notes: Direct successor to ACM DEV; explicitly interdisciplinary (CS, social sciences, environment, economics) and a core venue for ICTD/COMPASS-style work. Pipeline uses DBLP proceedings pages (ACM DL blocks bots).

years.note: resolve via DBLP index when compass2025.html appears

venues.name: ACM International Conference on Information and Communication Technologies and Development (ICTD)
venues.notes: The flagship ICTD conference; not held every year (roughly biennial in recent years -- 2020, 2022, 2024). No edition in 2021, 2023, or 2025. Pipeline uses DBLP proceedings pages.

years.note: just outside the 5-year window but included for continuity

venues.name: ACM Journal on Computing and Sustainable Societies (JCSS)
venues.notes: New quarterly journal launched November 2023 as the journal counterpart to COMPASS/ICTD; editor Lakshminarayanan Subramanian (NYU). Pipeline uses DBLP journal volume pages.

venues.name: AAAI Special Track on AI for Social Impact (AISI)
venues.notes: Distinct review rubric from the AAAI main track, emphasizing fit between technique and a socially important problem rather than pure technical novelty. Pipeline scrapes the AISI section from the linked OJS issue pages.

years.issue_note: AISI combined into 'IAAI-22, EAAI-22, AAAI-22 Special Programs and Special Track' issue
AISI combined into 'AAAI-23 Special Tracks' issue (with Safe/Robust AI track)
Vol 38 No. 20: AAAI-24 Special Track AI for Social Impact, Senior Member Presentations, New Faculty Highlights, Journal Track
Vol 39 No. 27: AAAI-25 Special Track on AI for Social Impact, Senior Member Presentations, New Faculty Highlights, Journal Track
Vol 40 No. 45-46: AAAI-26 Special Track AI for Social Impact I and II
years.note: just outside strict 5-year window but included since it is the newest edition

venues.name: ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining (KDD)
venues.notes: KDD does not currently have a separate ACM DL proceedings track specifically titled 'Social Impact' -- social-impact-themed work is published within the main Research/Applied Data Science (ADS) track proceedings. Pipeline uses DBLP proceedings pages; keyword/topic filtering still needed.

related_workshop.name: KDD Workshop on Data Science for Social Good (DSSG)

years.note: multi-volume year; resolved from DBLP index

venues.name: ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT)
venues.notes: Formerly FAT*; core venue for algorithmic fairness, accountability, sociotechnical audit work. Pipeline uses DBLP proceedings pages.

venues.name: Climate Change AI (CCAI) Workshop Papers
venues.sponsor: Climate Change AI (non-profit; workshop papers hosted alongside ICML/NeurIPS/ICLR/AAAI)
venues.notes: Aggregates accepted papers from CCAI's 'Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning' workshops. Filterable by venue and by subject area on the single page; no separate per-year URLs.

venues.name: AAAI/ACM Conference on AI, Ethics, and Society (AIES)
venues.why_relevant: Directly relevant to socio-technical dynamics / algorithmic harms topic; overlaps heavily with FAccT authorship community but AAAI/ACM co-sponsored.
venues.notes: Pipeline uses AAAI OJS issue pages. 2025 spans three parts; 2021-2023 issue URLs not yet in venue_configs.

venues.name: IFIP WG 9.4 International Conference on the Implications of Information and Digital Technologies for Development (ICT4D)
venues.sponsor: International Federation for Information Processing (IFIP), Working Group 9.4
venues.publisher: Springer, IFIP Advances in Information and Communication Technology (IFIP AICT) series
venues.why_relevant: The longest-running dedicated ICT4D conference series (running since 1989); explicitly focused on social implications of ICT in developing regions.

venues.name: Information Technologies & International Development (ITID)
venues.why_relevant: Long-running (since 2003), open-access, closely tied to the ACM ICTD research community; frequently cross-published/cited alongside ICTD conference papers.
venues.notes: Pipeline uses legacy OJS 2 issue pages (table TOC). More recent issues still need lookup if wanted.

venues.name: Development Engineering: The Journal of Engineering in Economic Development
venues.why_relevant: Founded by UC Berkeley (CEGA) specifically to bridge engineering and development economics; open access; strong editorial board.
venues.status: DISCONTINUED as of 2023 -- no new issues, but the 2015-2023 archive remains on ScienceDirect and is worth mining for problem_discovery / cs_fundamentals / impact_evaluation readings.
venues.notes: Pipeline uses ScienceDirect volume supplement URLs; OpenAlex fallback when ScienceDirect blocks bots.

venues.name: IEEE Global Humanitarian Technology Conference (GHTC)
venues.why_relevant: IEEE-sponsored, genuinely international (rotates across US/global sites), focused on humanitarian and development-relevant engineering -- meets the 'IEEE + international' credibility bar.
venues.notes: Pipeline uses DBLP proceedings pages (IEEE Xplore is JS-rendered).

venues.name: ACM CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems
venues.why_relevant: Not a dedicated ICTD venue, but CHI publishes a substantial and growing volume of HCI4D / Global South work each year. Pipeline uses DBLP CHI index (resolves chiYYYY.html / chiYYYYa.html per year).

venues.name: ACM Conference on Computer-Supported Cooperative Work and Social Computing (CSCW)
venues.why_relevant: Published as part of PACM HCI (two issues/year, tagged CSCW1 and CSCW2); has a recurring Global South / ICTD-adjacent subset but is not dedicated to development. Pipeline uses DBLP PACM HCI volume pages with section filters.

venues.name: IJCAI Multi-Year Special Track on AI and Social Good
venues.sponsor: IJCAI (International Joint Conferences on Artificial Intelligence Organization)
venues.notes: Distinct review rubric emphasizing UN SDG relevance and civil-society collaboration. 2022 pipeline uses DBLP 'AI for Good' section; 2023-2024 use accepted-papers listing pages.

venues_considered_and_not_yet_researched.name: NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks track / general NeurIPS social-impact papers outside Climate Change AI workshop
venues_considered_and_not_yet_researched.reason: Very high volume, general ML venue -- only the Climate Change AI workshop subset was scoped in this pass; a full NeurIPS sweep was judged too broad for this iteration.
